Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We are given a description of a process and need to construct a Log Skeleton as a Python dictionary with specific keys

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The process is an order fulfillment process with the following activities in a typical sequence though constraints might not force a strict total order Activities RO CA RI PI QC PO GSL D IC RP

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: We will assume a straightforward process with the following typical sequence Receive Order Check Availability Reserve Items Pick Items Quality Check Pack Order Generate Shipping Label Dispatch Invoice Customer Record Payment

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The constraints are about what must happen if certain activities occur We need to derive the constraints that are logically implied by the process description

Name: Inductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Typically such a process has a normal flow but there might be alternative paths or error handling However the problem does not mention any exceptions

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We assume all orders are available and processed

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Lets assume that in the logs we have every case contains all activities exactly once in the order RO CA RI PI QC PO GSL D IC RP

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: If all activities occur exactly once then any two activities occur the same number of times so equivalence holds for all pairs

Name: Validation - IND
Text: But wait there might be activities that do not occur in every case For example if an order is canceled after CA But not mentioned We will assume every case has all activities

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Since each occurs exactly once per case the activity frequency for each activity is 1

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: In a linear sequence for any two activities where one comes later if the later one occurs then the earlier one has occurred previously so always before includes all such ordered pairs

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Similarly in a linear sequence if one activity comes earlier then the later one will occur afterward so always after includes all such ordered pairs

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Since all activities occur in every case there are no pairs that never co exist so never together is empty

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The directly follows constraints are the consecutive pairs in the sequence RO CA CA RI RI PI PI QC QC PO PO GSL GSL D D IC IC RP

Name: Validation - IND
Text: Could there be cases where payment is recorded much later or other administrative activities happen in between Not mentioned so we keep the consecutive sequence as directly follows

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The final dictionary therefore contains equivalence as all unordered pairs always before as all later earlier pairs always after as all earlier later pairs never together as empty activity frequencies as 1 for all activities and directly follows as the adjacent sequence pairs
